chunks.text: Form No: HCJD/C-121 ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, MULTAN BENCH MULTAN JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Writ Petition No. 1056 of 2018 Mst. Zaib-ul-Nisa vs Judge Family Court etc S.No. of Order/ Proceeding Date of order/ proceeding Order with signature of Judge and that of parties or counsel where necessary 31.05.2018 Malik Zafar Mehboob Langrial, Advocate for petitioner. Malik Muhammad Farooq, Kamboh, Advocate for respondents. Through this constitutional petition, the petitioner has challenged the order dated 15.12.2017, passed by learned Senior Civil Judge/ Guardian Judge, Multan, whereby her application for production of additional evidence has been dismissed. 2. It is observed that on 16.09.2017 additional issue No.7-A was framed and the court recorded statements of both the parties that they would rely upon their previous evidence for the said issue. It appears that the petitioner herself made a statement that she would rely upon her previous evidence while framing the aforesaid additional issue and simultaneously filed an application for production of additional evidence which has been 2 WP No. 1056 of 2018 Dated 31.05.2018. dismissed by the trial court. The said order in interlocutory in nature and the petitioner may challenge the same while seeking remedy in case a final order is ultimately passed against her. 3. Resultantly, at this stage, no ground to interfere in the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court is made out. In view of the above, this petition is dismissed. (MUZAMIL AKHTAR SHABIR) JUDGE Naveed *
